page: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 design running at 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most multi-core and memory operations, with a notable strength in large-block read-only memory bandwidth, which reached 178.7 GB/sec. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 955.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.36 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression at 63.07 GB/sec and Geekbench software compilation at 25,264.0, align with average tier expectations. The server lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX, but includes hardware-accelerated AES cryptography.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARM-based architecture offers a resource-dense profile with dedicated physical cores, avoiding hyperthreading overhead. This makes it a balanced option for workloads that benefit from consistent multi-core execution without requiring high single-thread performance. The server is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, redis caching, and multi-threaded software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 AWS Graviton processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, 128.0 GB of memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core and database performance, with a strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 178.7 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is weaker, with lower scores in single-threaded compression and text processing. Lacking x86 instruction sets like AVX, it relies on ARM-native capabilities and dedicated cores to deliver stable execution. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, database operations, caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for multi-threaded web serving and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is an arm64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose server featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and balanced memory for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 178.7 GB/sec
Lacks local storage, GPU acceleration, and x86 vector instructions
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded compilation
